Keywords: Build layouts that adapt to different screen sizes and orientations
Description: Use LayoutBuilder/MediaQuery for breakpoints; test on multiple screen sizes; use flexible widgets
Do: Hardcode pixel sizes; assume one screen size; ignore landscape
Code Good:
Container(
  width: 375, // Only works on iPhone SE
  height: 812, // Hardcoded for one device
  child: ...
)

Keywords: Make app usable with screen readers and assistive technologies
Description: Add semantic labels; ensure contrast ratios; minimum tap targets 48x48; test with VoiceOver/TalkBack
Do: Rely only on color for information; small touch targets; missing labels
Dont:
Semantics(
  label: 'Add to cart',
  hint: 'Adds this product to your shopping cart',
  button: true,
  child: IconButton(
    icon: const Icon(Icons.add_shopping_cart),
    onPressed: () => addToCart(product),
    padding: const EdgeInsets.all(12), // 48x48 minimum
  ),
)

// Sufficient contrast
TextStyle(
  color: Colors.black87, // 4.5:1 contrast on white
  fontSize: 16, // Minimum readable size
)

Keywords: Follow Material Design 3 guidelines for consistent Android-first design
Description: Use MaterialApp theme with ColorScheme.fromSeed; use M3 components; follow elevation system
Do: Mix Material 2 and 3; ignore dynamic color; use custom colors without system
Dont:
MaterialApp(
  theme: ThemeData(
    useMaterial3: true,
    colorScheme: ColorScheme.fromSeed(
      seedColor: const Color(0xFF6750A4),
      brightness: Brightness.light,
    ),
    typography: Typography.material2021(),
  ),
  darkTheme: ThemeData(
    useMaterial3: true,
    colorScheme: ColorScheme.fromSeed(
      seedColor: const Color(0xFF6750A4),
      brightness: Brightness.dark,
    ),
  ),
)

Keywords: Support both light and dark themes with proper contrast and imagery
Description: Define both light and dark themes; use theme colors not hardcoded; adapt images/icons
Do: Hardcode colors; ignore dark mode; use white backgrounds everywhere
Code Good:
Container(
  color: Colors.white, // Blinding in dark mode
  child: Text('Hello', style: TextStyle(color: Colors.black)),
)

Description: Use .adaptive constructors; follow platform conventions; test on both iOS and Android
Do: Force Material design on iOS; ignore platform navigation patterns

Keywords: Use consistent spacing and typography scales
Description: Define spacing constants; use theme typography; maintain vertical rhythm
Do: Use random spacing values; mix font sizes without system

Keywords: Show appropriate loading states for async content
Description: Use skeleton screens; show progress indicators; handle empty states
Do: Show blank screens; block UI with modal loading; show nothing while loading

Description: Use bottom navigation for 3-5 top-level destinations; use drawer for 5+; handle back button properly
Do: Use bottom nav with 2 or 7+ items; inconsistent back behavior; deep nesting
Dont:
NavigationBar(
  selectedIndex: _currentIndex,
  onDestinationSelected: (index) => setState(() => _currentIndex = index),
  destinations: const [
    NavigationDestination(icon: Icon(Icons.home), label: 'Home'),
    NavigationDestination(icon: Icon(Icons.search), label: 'Search'),
    NavigationDestination(icon: Icon(Icons.shopping_cart), label: 'Cart'),
    NavigationDestination(icon: Icon(Icons.person), label: 'Profile'),
  ],
)

Keywords: Show user-friendly error messages with actionable options
Description: Show contextual error messages; provide retry actions; use appropriate formats (snackbar/banner/dialog)
Do: Show raw exception text; show errors as blocking dialogs for non-critical issues

Keywords: Design intuitive touch interactions following platform conventions
Description: Use standard gestures; provide haptic feedback; ensure adequate touch targets; support pull-to-refresh
Do: Create non-standard gestures; rely on gestures without visual affordance

Keywords: Use purposeful animations that enhance UX not distract
Description: Keep animations under 300ms; use standard easing curves; hero transitions for context; reduce motion option
Do: Overuse animations; long durations; animations without purpose

Keywords: Design user-friendly forms with proper input types and validation
Description: Use correct keyboard types; enable autofill; show inline validation; group related fields
Do: Use generic keyboard for all inputs; validate only on submit; no autofill hints
Dont:
TextFormField(
  keyboardType: TextInputType.emailAddress,
  textInputAction: TextInputAction.next,
  autofillHints: const [AutofillHints.email],
  decoration: InputDecoration(
    labelText: 'Email',
    prefixIcon: Icon(Icons.email),
    errorText: _emailError,
  ),
  onChanged: _validateEmail,
)

// Auto-advance to next field
TextFormField(
  textInputAction: TextInputAction.next,
  onFieldSubmitted: (_) => FocusScope.of(context).nextFocus(),
)
